reforms.description: The Australian Government released a package of measures costing A$ 1.3 billion aimed at improving industry performance, which includes increased support for business research and development and the commercialization of that research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased support for R&D and commercialization is expected to enhance industry performance and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been released and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant investment in R&D funding aimed at improving industry performance.
reforms.source_quote: The Government released its A$ 1.3 billion Investing for Growth industry statement as part of its extensive reform agenda to deliver improved industry performance.

reforms.description: The Australian government introduced a scheme providing a bonus for individuals who defer taking up the age pension and remain in full-time employment, aimed at encouraging later retirement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging later retirement can help maintain a skilled workforce and support economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been introduced and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in its aim to influence workforce participation but is not as large-scale as the R&D funding measures.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced in July 1998 a scheme which provides a bonus to persons who remain in full-time employment and defer taking up the age pension.
